cards.back: A bridge between the JDBC API and the ODBC API, allowing JDBC to access databases that don't support JDBC

cards.front: What type of databases can be accessed through the JDBC/ODBC Bridge?
cards.back: Databases that don't support JDBC, such as Microsoft Access

cards.front: What is the purpose of the JDBC/ODBC Bridge in Java?
cards.back: To allow Java programs to access databases that don't support JDBC

cards.front: What is the role of the JDBC/ODBC Bridge in the database access process?
cards.back: To translate JDBC API calls into ODBC API calls, allowing access to the database

cards.front: What is the advantage of using the JDBC/ODBC Bridge in Java?
cards.back: To provide a way to access a wide range of databases from a single Java program
